title: A flexible printed circuit board
abstract: A flexible printed circuit board (100) comprises a printed conductor (101) and at least one component (102) forming an interface region (103) with said flexible printed circuit board. The conductor (101) elongates through the interface region (103), wherein stiffness of said component (101) is greater than stiffness of the flexible printed circuit board (100) thereby causing mechanical discontinuity point on said conductor at the interface region (103). The flexible printed circuit board (100) is configured (105) to stiffen in the direction (104) of the conductor (101) towards said component (102) at least in the interface region (103) in order to eliminate or minimize said mechanical discontinuity point.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A flexible substrate (100) comprising a conductor (101 ) and additionally at least one component (102) forming an interface region (103) with said flexible substrate, said conductor (101 ) elongating through said interface region (103), wherein stiffness of said component (102) is greater than stiffness of the flexible substrate (100) thereby causing mechanical discontinuity point on said conductor at the interface region (103), and wherein said flexible substrate (100) is configured to stiffen in the direction of the conductor towards said component (102) at least in the interface region (103) characterized in that - the flexible printed circuit board is configured to gradually stiffen at least in the direction (104) of the said conductor (101 ) towards said component (102), or - the flexible printed circuit board (100) is configured to continuously stiffen at least in the direction (104) of said conductor (101 ) towards said component (102). 2. A flexible printed circuit board of claim 1 , wherein the flexible printed circuit board comprises number of different size integrated stiffening portions (105) next to said component (102) arranged so that the stiffness of said flexible printed circuit board (100) stiffens in the direction (104) of the conductor (101 ) towards said component (102). 3. A flexible printed circuit board of any of previous claims, wherein the flexible printed circuit board comprises number of different radius circular portions (105) around said component (102) arranged so that the stiffness of said flexible printed circuit board (100) stiffens in the direction (104) of said conductor (101 ) towards said component (102). 4. A flexible printed circuit board of any of previous claims, wherein the stiffness of said stiffening portions (105) is less that stiffness of said component (102). 5. A flexible printed circuit board of any of previous claims, wherein said stiffening portions (105) are laminated or glued or welded to the flexible printed circuit. 6. A flexible printed circuit board of any of previous claims, wherein said 

 flexible printed circuit board comprises said stiffening portions (105) either on one side of said flexible printed circuit, preferable on the same side as said conductor, or on the opposite side as said conductor or on the both sides of the flexible printed circuit. 7. A flexible printed circuit board of any of previous claims, wherein said stiffening portions (105) comprises polyethylene terephthalate (PET) material, TPU, polyimide (PI). 8. A flexible printed circuit board of any of previous claims, wherein the flexible substrate (100) comprises thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU) material, PET, polyimide (PI). 9. A flexible printed circuit board of any of previous claims, wherein at least one of said discontinuity points is located in the interface region (103) of said component (102) and the printed conductor (101 ). 10. A flexible printed circuit board of any of previous claims, wherein said component (102) is selected from a group comprising: an integrated circuit (IC), LED, connector, a rigid circuit board (FR4), housing, electric component, transmitter, antenna, battery, power source, switch, and button. 1 1. A method for manufacturing a flexible printed circuit board (100) of any of previous claims, said flexible printed circuit board comprising a printed conductor and additionally at least one component (102) coupled with said printed conductor, wherein stiffness of said component is greater than stiffness of the flexible printed circuit board thereby causing mechanical discontinuity points along a trace of said printed conductor, wherein the method comprises configuring (105) said flexible printed circuit board to stiffen in the direction (104) of the printed conductor (101 ) towards said component (102) characterized in that the method comprises additionally steps of - providing the flexible printed circuit board to gradually stiffen at least in the direction (104) of the said conductor (101 ) towards said component (102), or - providing the flexible printed circuit board (100) to continuously stiffen at least in the direction (104) of said conductor (101 ) towards said component (102). 12. A method of claim 1 1 , wherein the method comprises providing the 

 flexible printed circuit board by number of different size integrated stiffening portions (105) next to said component (102) and arranging said integrated stiffening portions (105) so that the stiffness of said flexible printed circuit board (100) stiffens in the direction (104) of said conductor (101 ) towards said component (102). 13. A method of any of claims 1 1 or 12, wherein the method comprises laminating or gluing or welding stiffening portions (105) to the flexible printed circuit. 14. A method of any of claims 1 1 -13, wherein the method comprises integrating stiffening portions (105) either on one side of said flexible printed circuit, preferable on the same side as said printed conductor, or on the opposite side as said printed conductor or on the both sides of the flexible printed circuit.
